mentions.note: Space stock referenced in context of current news cycle. Cramer doesn't know ticker or company name; will research and follow up.

IPO'd today at $20, closed $19.01; outsourced mfg for space/defense (like Flex/Jabil/Celestica but military-focused). $1.06B backlog = 1.75x revenue; EBITDA margins low 20s. 31x 2026E multiple slightly elevated but story likely cheaper than it looks. Prefer mid-teens entry but okay to buy here—not chasing, valuation not crazy. Greenbrier retains 81%.

B2B AI play with lucrative revenue streams; if only big IPO of 2026 would be all-in; sandwiched between SpaceX and OpenAI reduces enthusiasm; concerns about supply glut and potential underpricing in crowded queue.

Caller position: bought at $300, rode to $880 Aug, now down 45%. Cramer: hold it. Products doing well but P/E multiple too high (multiple is 'secret sauce' and too elevated); don't sell; wait for multiple compression.

Shouldn't trade with geopolitical events but does (market perception: war will hurt travel demand). Don't touch. Can't buy until stock goes lower or war ends. Stuck in purgatory with other conflict-sensitive names.
Selling at lowest level-to-market multiple Cramer's ever seen. Very inexpensive. Buy some and wait to see if further oversold. Strong buy on valuation metrics alone.

Cloud storage platform (Nasdaq-listed); caller does homework and personally likes technology. Cramer unfamiliar with company; will research with team analyst Ben Stoto before providing recommendation.

Record order volume but profitability lagging; sells at premium 32x earnings multiple. Tony Xu built #1 company in field; like management very much; prefer entry at 100-120 range vs current premium valuation; wait for pullback.
Software development good business but multiple too high. Take some profits off table tomorrow morning; trim position. Valuation needs reset.
Caller asked about post-merger with property sales for kids' portfolio. Cramer: too dicey; don't want to sour kids on stocks; many better REIT alternatives available; too much risk and debt concerns.

Core holding for 5 years, up 360%; revenue CAGR 25% to $65.2B; EPS growth to $24.21. Retatrutide breakthrough (70 lb weight loss potential), gene therapy for cholesterol (VERVE-102), three vaccine acquisitions ($3.8B), CVS PBM deal expanding access. CEO David Ricks excellent steward. 'Biggest drug of all time' potential; sticking with Lilly for long haul.

Core holding in trust; remains believer in AI and Jensen Huang long-term; but likely to become 'market donor' as portfolio managers sell to raise capital for IPOs; expect tough time for shareholders near-term; own through rough patch and wait for buyback opportunities as buyers make fortunes.

Private company losing tons of money; IPO timing uncertain; could be real negative surprise if comes last in capital raise queue; not hearing signals it's ready for public markets; funding profile concerning.

Logistics REIT pivoting to data center with 5.6 GW power and 1.3 GW solar (only 8% of roofs done; huge runway). Core portfolio sold out; e-commerce tailwinds (uses 3x warehouse space). $750M mark-to-market organic growth opportunity on 11k leases. New CEO Dan Letter continues excellence culture. 3% yield; only REIT to bottom in 2008 recession; long-time fan.
Had huge run last month (close to triple based on Cramer's prior recommendation). Stock paused to catch breath. Don't sell it. If comes back to 10, buy more. Strong pullback buyer.

Cramer enthusiastic about SpaceX and Starlink long-term; believes in space opportunity and Musk's ability to create wealth. Cautions retail investors: use limit orders only at IPO open, not market orders; expect opening price ~$270 (2x offer price); book runners should ban market orders and release lockup sellers to prevent runaway $4T+ valuation.

GTA 6 coming out; CEO Strauss Zelnick has a real winner; probably biggest entertainment property of all time. Stock down 6% on day; erratic trader; don't buy all at once but represents good entry point for patient investors.
